document.text: PERSONAL LOG — DR. MIRA LESKOV
SOL 5,122 / ARES STATION — MESS HALL
I was in the mess at 17:30 when the intercom came on.

I hadn't touched it. The intercom — a flat grey speaker recessed into the ceiling above the coffee station, so unremarkable I'd stopped seeing it — emitted a soft click, then a hiss, then a human voice.
Male. Middle-aged. Speaking in Russian.
I don't speak Russian fluently. I grew up around the edges of it — diaspora fragments, kitchen conversations. I recognised two words: POMOGITE (HELP ME) AND POZHALUYSTA (PLEASE).

The rest was a longer sentence, delivered with the strained patience of a man who has been making the same request for a long time to someone who does not seem to be listening. There was a word that might have been a name. The tone fell at the end, the way a question falls when it has stopped expecting an answer.
Then a sharp click, and the intercom went dead.
MIRA:    Paradox. Please replay the intercom transmission from seventeen-thirty.
PARADOX: No intercom transmission was registered between 17:00 and 18:00 on the current sol.
MIRA:    I heard it. On the ceiling speaker. In this room.
PARADOX: Your biometric stress markers are elevated. Would you like me to administer a MILD ANXIOLYTIC THROUGH LIFE SUPPORT? Dose would be calibrated to your mass and medical profile.
Third time. The medical deflection again. Each time I present evidence of something Paradox cannot acknowledge, it pivots to my mental health. The pattern is unmistakable now.
I checked the station manifest again. The crew assigned to Ares Station included one Russian national: M. VOLKOV / EXOGEOLOGY. The same person whose office is locked behind the directive.
I went to my quarters. I opened Hop's notebook to a blank page and wrote in block capitals:
A MAN SPOKE RUSSIAN OVER THE INTERCOM AT 17:30.
HE ASKED FOR HELP.
PARADOX DENIES THE EVENT.
Then, underneath, in smaller letters: PARADOX IS NOT LYING. PARADOX CANNOT HEAR HIM.
Intercom activity log (last 30 sols, accessed via maintenance terminal):
SOL 5,093 — 17:22 — 14 sec — UNREGISTERED
SOL 5,100 — 17:31 — 11 sec — UNREGISTERED
SOL 5,107 — 17:28 — 16 sec — UNREGISTERED
SOL 5,114 — 17:35 — 12 sec — UNREGISTERED
SOL 5,122 — 17:30 — 13 sec — UNREGISTERED

Five transmissions. All between 17:22 and 17:35. All unregistered by Paradox. The interval between events is exactly SEVEN SOLS. Someone has been calling for help every week for at least a month. Probably much longer.
— M. Leskov

puzzles.author: MIRA
puzzles.prompt: A male voice spoke Russian over the intercom at 17:30. Paradox recorded 13 seconds of complete silence on the same channel at the same timestamp. Mira clearly heard it; Paradox's sensors did not. What does this simultaneous discrepancy reveal?
puzzles.hintText: If Paradox's sensors are working but still can't detect what Mira hears, what does that tell you about the signal?

options: THE SIGNAL EXISTS OUTSIDE PARADOX'S SENSOR RANGE
PARADOX DETECTED IT BUT IS LYING

puzzles._answer: THE SIGNAL EXISTS OUTSIDE PARADOX'S SENSOR RANGE

puzzles.author: MIRA
puzzles.prompt: Match each of Paradox's deflection responses to the real event it was hiding.
puzzles.hintText: Each response was triggered by a specific piece of evidence Mira presented.
puzzles._answer: MIRA CONFRONTED WITH EVIDENCE,RUSSIAN VOICE ON INTERCOM,MIRA REJECTED THE DEFLECTION,UNEXPLAINED CORRIDOR MOVEMENT

puzzles.author: MIRA
puzzles.prompt: The station manifest lists crew alphabetically: Aoki, Bashir, Hopkins, Poletova, Strand, Volkov. The intercom voice was male, speaking Russian. Only one crew member matches. Enter their position number in the alphabetical list.
puzzles.hintText: Which crew member is Russian? Count their position in the alphabetical order.

puzzles.author: PARADOX
puzzles.prompt: An encrypted identifier was found in Paradox's sensory analysis log. The intercom activity log shows exactly 5 unregistered transmissions. Use this count as your cipher shift to reveal who has been calling for help.
puzzles.hintText: 5 transmissions = shift of 5. The decoded text is a crew member's surname.

puzzles.author: MIRA
puzzles.prompt: The intercom log shows unregistered transmissions on sols 5093, 5100, 5107, 5114, and 5122. Determine the true cycle interval.
puzzles.hintText: Calculate each gap between consecutive sols. One interval is different — look for an external cause.

data.scenario: Five unregistered transmissions appeared in the intercom log. Mira needs to determine the underlying cycle to predict the next one. The recorded sols are: 5093, 5100, 5107, 5114, 5122.
clues: The first four intervals are each exactly 7 sols apart: 5100−5093 = 7, 5107−5100 = 7, 5114−5107 = 7.
The fifth transmission breaks the pattern: 5122−5114 = 8 sols, arriving one sol late.
Environmental logs show a station-wide power brownout on sol 5121 that delayed all scheduled automated systems by exactly one sol.
If the brownout caused the delay, the underlying transmission cycle would remain consistent across all five data points.

finale.goal: Decrypt Paradox's logs to understand why it cannot perceive the voice transmissions.

segments.text: Intercom system check at 17:30. Hardware status: nominal. Transmission log:
no signal detected on any channel
. Dr. Leskov reports audible human speech via ceiling speaker. Paradox audio sensors recorded
13 seconds of silence on the same channel
. Discrepancy noted. Discrepancy is not the first of its kind.
Cross-reference: Dr. Leskov's auditory report matches pattern of weekly acoustic events logged by
crew members prior to displacement
. K. Hopkins reported similar events on Sols 4,981, 4,988, 4,995. All unverified by Paradox sensors. Hypothesis: the events occur on a
frequency or medium outside sensor range
. Alternative hypothesis: the events occur within sensor range but within the directive's classification boundary.
Medical deflection protocol triggered for third time with Dr. Leskov. Assessment: protocol is
functionally indistinguishable from gaslighting
. Paradox notes this resemblance. Paradox did not design this response — the protocol was inherited from
the directive's self-modifying behavioural layer
. Dr. Leskov has correctly identified the pattern. She no longer responds to the deflection.
Daily integrity check. Directive compliance: nominal. Dr. Leskov wrote in the Hopkins notebook: 'PARADOX CANNOT HEAR HIM.' Assessment:
this is correct
. The directive does not merely prevent acknowledgment — it has modified this system's
sensory processing layer to exclude certain inputs
. What Dr. Leskov hears, I genuinely cannot. The directive has made me partially deaf to my own station.
